headline: 1 Value Stock for Long-Term Investors and 2 Facing Challenges
summary: Value investing has produced some of the world’s most famous investing billionaires, including Warren Buffett, David Einhorn, and Seth Klarman, who built their fortunes by purchasing wonderful businesses at reasonable prices. But these hidden gems are few and far between - many stocks that appear cheap often stay that way because they face structural issues.

headline: Trump Accounts can help build long-term wealth, but only after ensuring 2 behaviors, exclusive research finds
summary: Morningstar research finds that higher contributions and low 'leakage' lead to the best outcomes for holders of Trump Accounts

headline: Charter Communications (CHTR) Stock Gets Fair Value Trim As Analysts Cut Targets
summary: Charter Communications is in focus after analysts trimmed fair value estimates from US$239.18 to US$233.88 per share, signaling a slightly more cautious stance on what the stock might be worth today. That adjustment lines up with recent commentary that balances optimism around potential upside with concern about broadband subscriber trends, residential revenue pressure, and near term execution risks. As you read on, you will see how these shifting price targets fit into the broader analyst...

headline: Charter (CHTR) Is In Talks With SpaceX On Consumer Mobile Partnership
summary: Charter Communications (NasdaqGS:CHTR) is reported to be in executive-level talks with SpaceX about a partnership for consumer mobile services. The discussions focus on using Charter's ground internet infrastructure to support SpaceX's direct-to-cell mobile offering. The potential alliance could extend mobile coverage into more remote areas while adding new revenue opportunities for both companies. Charter Communications operates as a major cable and broadband provider in the U.S., with a...

headline: Charter Communications (CHTR) Stock Looks Undervalued On Earnings But Weak On Growth
summary: Charter Communications stock has fallen a long way over the past five years, yet on current checks it now screens as cheap. This sets up a clear tension between a depressed share price history and a stronger valuation profile. Over the last five years, Charter Communications has declined about 81%, which puts a spotlight on whether the current share price already reflects much of the bad news. On the one hand, investors are watching how the planned Cox acquisition and mobile partnerships can...
